creator: hardy.schumacher@gmx.de
text:
Created attachment 150609
startx console output

After upgrading my system to FreeBSD-10.1 (i386), removal of all ports and new installation of all required ports, startx hangs.
I'm using xorg and enlightenment, X is started by using startx command from console.
Removing the content of ${HOME}/.e and ${HOME}/.elementary did not solve the problem.
Xorg/Enlightenment hangs at initialization phase, when "Connman" is initialized: it reports "Connman support disabled" and after click on "Next" button nothing happens.
So I had to kill X manually.

The attached logfile contains the content of the console output. Logfile /var/log/Xorg.0.log is not showing any issues.
